tensions.actual: v1.4.0: Conceptual foundation mature — standard of measurement principle, operative identity clarified. CLI reading surface consolidated to 4 primary commands (show/list/tree/stats) with rich filtering. MCP gains stats tool (8 sections). Output taxonomy settled. Three interface surfaces operational: TUI (survey V1.3), CLI (consolidated reading surface), MCP (31 tools). Cooperative dynamics remain unexplored (pending Foundations course April 2026).
tensions.desired: werk is a mature tool for practicing structural dynamics — individually and cooperatively

tensions.actual: V1-V8 deck view complete. Survey view (V1) shipped in v1.3.0: Tab/Shift+Tab pivot/return yaw, inherited horizons with depth-first tree grouping, zone compression, sticky band+provider headers, per-line rendering, right-aligned closure ratios, gestures from survey. Three rendering bugs fixed (⏱ width, bold SGR bleed, scroll diff thrashing). Remaining: threshold mechanics (#19), survey tree orphan fix (intermediate nodes scrolling off), frame controls, input point rethinking.
tensions.desired: werk is a FrankenTUI-first application where the terminal interface is the primary experience

tensions.actual: Authority, provenance, and multi-player identity surfaced as design questions through community issue evaluation. The operative-not-managerial distinction is now canonized in the conceptual foundation — werk serves operations (closing gaps), not management (coordinating existing structure). This sharpens the multi-participant question: cooperation in werk means shared commitment to closing gaps honestly, not coordination topology. The addressing/sessions/gestures exploration (designs/addressing-sessions-gestures.md) is where the multi-player foundation lives — sessions carry authorship, gestures carry provenance, deep addressability makes everything referenceable across participants.
tensions.desired: a clear structural model exists for how multiple participants hold and evolve shared tension fields

tensions.actual: course is scheduled for end of April 2026; no direct input yet from the structural consulting methodology on cooperative dynamics
tensions.desired: the Foundations for Structural Thinking course has been completed and its insights on collective structure are integrated into the cooperative model

tensions.actual: werk flush command writes tensions.json to workspace root — a git-trackable JSON file containing all tensions as raw structural data; .werk/ remains gitignored; consumers can reconstruct the tree and compute dynamics from the flushed data without the CLI
tensions.desired: workspace state is visible in git diffs and PRs without requiring the CLI

tensions.actual: Note commands restructured as noun-verb subcommands (note add/rm/list). Retraction via note_retracted mutation. All tests pass. Ready to resolve.
tensions.desired: Note deletion capability: delete or soft-delete notes that were added to the wrong tension

tensions.actual: Interactive testing complete. Gaze card, help screen, grab-and-drop reordering, temporal alignment, alert truncation, empty descended state, rules width all verified and working. Unicode display width vs char count issue in selected tension background highlight remains. Three-agent design system generation process completed — independent design documents and reflections collected from Claude, Gemini, and Codex for synthesis into definitive visual coherence plan.
tensions.desired: rendering rebuild is visually verified and interaction-tested

tensions.actual: CLI reading surface consolidated: 10 commands → 4 primary (show, list, tree, stats) + 1 named perspective (survey). list is the general-purpose query engine with 12 filter/sort flags. stats replaces ground/insights/trajectory/health with 8 section flags. show --full absorbs context. tree --stats appends vitals. MCP surface mirrors CLI: new stats tool, enriched show/list schemas. Old commands hidden but functional. CLI help reflects operative identity. CLAUDE.md updated.
tensions.desired: CLI is a complete, honest interface to the structure — foundation vocabulary, readable output, structured JSON, every operative gesture available, no agent-specific orchestration commands

tensions.actual: CLI human-facing output fully aligned with foundation vocabulary: Reality (not Actual), Deadline (not Horizon) in show/add/compose-up/horizon commands. CLI aliases: werk actual → reality, werk deadline → horizon. --phase filter removed from list. 'phase transition' → 'epoch boundary'. Agent prompts updated. DynamicsEngine renamed to Engine. dynamics.rs consolidated into temporal.rs (sd-core), renamed to serialize.rs (CLI). JSON keys and struct fields unchanged (backward compatible). Remaining: TUI labels (blocked on TUI rebuild).
tensions.desired: consistent terminology across CLI, TUI, and agent-facing documentation — unambiguous names for the werkspace, its contents, and the operations on them

tensions.actual: Three design system documents (33KB Claude, 26KB Codex, 9KB Gemini) and three reflection documents collected in werk-design-inputs/. Two prior design docs also available. No synthesis started. Agent flywheel methodology (agent-flywheel.com) identified as process reference.
tensions.desired: the three independent design systems are synthesized into a single definitive design system that becomes the implementation blueprint

tensions.actual: Substantial implementation complete. Data model: gestures, sessions, epochs tables; gesture_id + actual_at on mutations; parent_snapshot_json on child creation. TUI fully wired into gesture/session model — every mutation is a session-bound gesture, epoch boundaries carry trigger gesture IDs. Temporal calculus: 6 computed properties in temporal.rs. Frontier projection: operating envelope in frontier.rs. Hook infrastructure: 5 hook types with JSON payloads. Pathway palettes: containment/sequencing signals independent of invocation. Advisory file locking for fsqlite safety. Epoch display in show output across all surfaces (CLI, MCP, TUI frontier). Output taxonomy decided and codified. Remaining gaps: formal state machine extraction (#16), structural transformations (#49), deep addressability (#128), user-defined thresholds (#139).
tensions.desired: the conceptual foundation is implemented — data model supports delta versioning, gesture grouping, order of operations distinct from horizon, held steps, and the five temporal concepts

tensions.actual: All speculative dynamics gutted. sd-core computes only honest facts: urgency (elapsed fraction of horizon), horizon drift (pattern of horizon changes), gap_magnitude (binary — has gap or not), temporal signals (implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path, containment violation), closure progress. Net -15,095 lines across 28 files. 881 tests pass. DynamicsEngine simplified to Store wrapper with parent snapshot capture. CLI JSON output drops dynamics field — shows temporal signals, urgency, overdue, closure instead. strsim dependency removed. TUI updated to render without speculative dynamics. Consider renaming sd-core to werk-core as optional follow-up.
tensions.desired: old dynamics code removed from sd-core — text-similarity magnitude, text-convergence resolution, oscillation, conflict, orientation, creative cycle phase, assimilation depth, compensating strategy, structural tendency all deleted. sd-core computes honest facts and signals only: urgency, temporal properties (implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path, containment), closure progress. No speculative dynamics remain. Consider renaming sd-core to werk-core.

tensions.actual: V1-V8 complete. Survey view (#18) V1 implemented: Tab/Shift+Tab pivot/return yaw, inherited horizons with depth-first tree grouping, zone compression, closure ratios, gestures from survey. Three-times framework (taxis/chronos/kairos) identified — potential third orientation (frontier/kairos view). Unicode width bug found and fixed (⏱ glyph removed from both deck and survey). Remaining on #18: sticky deadline header, NOW zone, frame controls, held steps treatment. Remaining on #15: threshold mechanics (#19), frame widening/narrowing.
tensions.desired: TUI rebuilt around the operating envelope as primary interaction surface — frontier-centered screen layout, pitch/roll/yaw navigation, threshold mechanics, descended/horizon view toggle, peek gestures, ground mode access

tensions.actual: State machine exists implicitly in TUI: InputMode enum has 8 states (Normal, Adding, Editing, Annotating, Confirming, Moving, Reordering, Searching, Help) plus focus zoom and peek as render-layer states. Transitions are implemented in update.rs key handlers. Not formally specified in XState/Stately — was built organically during V1-V8. The formal spec question is whether the current implicit machine needs to be extracted and validated, or whether the working implementation is sufficient.
tensions.desired: complete state machine specification — all states, transitions, key bindings per state, designed in XState/Stately before Rust implementation

tensions.actual: Calculus of time fully consolidated in temporal.rs: urgency, horizon drift, gap magnitude moved from dynamics.rs; implied window, sequencing pressure, critical path (recursive), containment violation already there. dynamics.rs deleted. DynamicsEngine renamed to Engine. CLI dynamics.rs renamed to serialize.rs. All imports updated. 859 tests pass.
tensions.desired: calculus of time implemented — implied execution window, urgency from deadline windows, sequencing pressure, critical path (recursive), containment violation detection

tensions.actual: Survey view V1.3: NOW zone (dim temporal separator between imminent and overdue, or at bottom when no overdue), field vitals in bottom bar. Held step treatment with position glyphs matching deck (green next-step, cyan route, subdued held). Inline breadcrumb ancestor pinning for orphaned tree connectors. Simplified right column (zero-padded ID only). Approaching band always expanded (min 5 items). Band headers padded to full width. Sticky band header tracks cursor band explicitly. Bayesian diff strategy disabled for deterministic rendering. clear_area_styled uses TRANSPARENT bg for light-mode compatibility. Light mode tension #101 created with decomposed steps #102-#106. Remaining: white glitch still intermittent, sticky header edge cases at band boundaries, render_survey growing.
tensions.desired: survey view designed and implemented — temporal framing, structural annotations, frame widening/narrowing, context-carrying yaw transition

tensions.actual: thresholds conceptually defined. No implementation. Hold-key detection feasibility untested across terminals
tensions.desired: threshold mechanics implemented — tap/hold navigation, lingering space visual treatment, screen boundary signals

tensions.actual: Schema fully extended: 3 new tables (gestures, sessions, epochs), 3 new columns (gesture_id + actual_at on mutations, parent_snapshot_json on tensions). Gesture context wired into Store with process-scoped session model. All CLI mutation commands create gestures. TUI fully wired — every mutation is a session-bound gesture, epoch boundaries carry trigger gesture IDs. Mutation replay tolerates note/position/deleted. CLI: werk hold, werk position, werk resolve --actual-at. Parent snapshots auto-populated as JSON on child creation. Epoch display in show output (CLI + MCP). All three surfaces consistent.
tensions.desired: data model extended — gesture grouping (gesture table), run/session tracking (run table), epoch markers, notes as first-class mutations, held vs positioned as explicit state

tensions.actual: CLI has 30+ commands. hold, position, compose-up, epoch, survey, and ground commands added. Old terminology cleaned: 'phase distribution' removed from health description, --phase filter removed from list, 'phase transition' → 'epoch boundary' in reality/desire output, 'actual state' → 'current reality' in help text, agent prompts updated. 'run' command still refers to agent invocation, not sessions (by design — sessions are TUI-scoped). Sort options updated to urgency/name/deadline.
tensions.desired: CLI commands aligned with foundation vocabulary — compose-up, hold/position, ground, survey, epoch marking, run tracking commands added; old commands updated to use new terminology

tensions.actual: All speculative dynamics removed from sd-core. dynamics.rs gutted from 9401 to 686 lines — keeps only Urgency, HorizonDrift, gap_magnitude (binary). engine.rs gutted from 2266 to 277 lines — keeps Store wrapper with parent snapshot capture, no dynamics computation. events.rs gutted from ~1050 to 507 lines — keeps state change events, removes 11 dynamics transition event types (ConflictDetected, LifecycleTransition, OscillationDetected, etc.). lib.rs exports cleaned (99 to 43 lines). strsim dependency removed. CLI dynamics.rs adapter gutted — removed DynamicsJson and all speculative types, keeps only TensionInfo/MutationInfo helpers. show/context/run/health/insights/watch commands updated to use honest facts only. JSON output drops dynamics field entirely. 881 tests pass, net -15,095 lines.
tensions.desired: old dynamics module gutted — all speculative dynamics (magnitude, resolution, oscillation, conflict, orientation, creative cycle phase, assimilation depth, compensating strategy, structural tendency) removed from sd-core. Only honest temporal facts and signals remain. DynamicsEngine simplified or removed. CLI dynamics.rs adapter cleaned up. JSON output updated to drop dynamics fields.

tensions.actual: Skills rewritten around foundation vocabulary and instrument/practice boundary. README rewritten covering three interface surfaces and MCP. Install section simplified. Remaining: no formal user guide, no crate-level reference docs (tracked separately as #51).
tensions.desired: skills and documentation updated to reflect foundation — SKILL.md, references, onboarding rewritten around new vocabulary and concepts

tensions.actual: watch daemon exists with 7 trigger types, pending insights stored in .werk/watch/pending/. No integration with run concept or ground mode. Triggers fire independently of session state
tensions.desired: watch daemon rethought — fits with sessions (session awareness), thresholds (triggers as threshold crossings), and ground mode (insights surface during debrief, not as interruptions)

tensions.actual: Pathway palettes V1 complete. Shared infrastructure in werk-shared/src/palette.rs (types, builders, applicators, detection helpers). CLI presentation in werk-cli/src/palette.rs (interactive, piped, JSON modes). Integrated into horizon (containment), position (sequencing), add (containment on child creation), and move (containment + sequencing on reparent). Option sets expanded: containment 5 options, sequencing 4 options. MCP surface: all four tools accept palette_response param for agent pre-selection. TUI integration tracked separately under #15/#58.
tensions.desired: pathway palettes in CLI — when a gesture creates a structural signal (containment violation, sequencing pressure, etc.), the instrument immediately presents response options inline. The user selects or dismisses. Synchronous instrument feedback within the gesture flow.

tensions.actual: parent_snapshot_json field exists on tensions. Auto-populated at child creation time in both store.create_tension_full() and engine.create_tension_with_parent(). Captures full JSON with desire, reality, status, horizon, and all children (id, desired, actual, status, position, horizon). parent_desired_snapshot and parent_actual_snapshot still populated for backward compat.
tensions.desired: parent snapshot captures full descended view state — not just desire/reality text but order of operations, held steps, resolved steps, children metadata

tensions.actual: batch command now groups all mutations under one gesture. begin_gesture called before apply loop, end_gesture after. Gesture description includes mutation count.
tensions.desired: batch command aligned with gesture grouping — batch applies a set of mutations as one gesture rather than individual atomic changes

tensions.actual: Dual-layer identity implemented. ULIDs remain canonical machine IDs. Workspace-local short_code (sequential integer, auto-assigned at creation, backfilled on migration) provides human addressing. PrefixResolver accepts both numeric short codes (exact match) and ULID prefixes (4+ chars). Short codes shown in werk show output (#N). Ambiguous prefix errors now show short codes. Zero collision risk for human addressing.
tensions.desired: the identity system is world-class and future-proof — IDs have strong prefix diversity for human addressing, zero collision risk at any resolution, and remain machine-sortable and globally unique

tensions.actual: actual_at field exists on mutations and is persisted. No CLI path to set it. Resolve command does not ask or accept when resolution actually happened.
tensions.desired: actual_at wired into resolve flow — werk resolve accepts --actual-at flag for 'I did this yesterday' cases, configurable prompt option per foundation calculus of time

tensions.actual: CLI trigger path complete (reality/desire commands auto-create epochs, --no-epoch for corrections, manual 'werk epoch' available). TUI trigger path complete: save_current_edit_field() calls close_epoch() on parent desire/reality edits, creating epoch snapshots. Only minor gap: TUI epochs don't carry gesture_id (passed as None).
tensions.desired: epoch creation has a trigger path — CLI or TUI prompts 'start a new epoch?' when desire/reality changes, snapshotting the current delta

tensions.actual: Sessions are process-scoped by design. begin_gesture() creates sessionless gestures (CLI, agent). begin_gesture_in_session(session_id) associates with a specific session (TUI). active_session() query still exists for TUI use, but CLI/agent paths never call it. Multiple TUI panes = multiple independent sessions. Agent mutations = sessionless gestures. This is architecturally settled. Remaining: TUI needs to call start_session on open and end_session on close.
tensions.desired: session lifecycle is clear — TUI instances manage their own sessions, CLI commands operate sessionless, agent mutations are sessionless gestures. Multiple concurrent TUI panes are independent sessions.

tensions.actual: Tree view now shows short codes (#N) as primary identity, theory of closure progress [R/T], OVERDUE flags, and status markers (✓/~). Old dynamics (phase badges, movement signals, conflict markers) removed. ULID prefixes no longer shown in tree — short codes are the human interface.
tensions.desired: tree view shows structural position numbering (1., 1.1., 1.1.3.) as orientation chrome alongside short codes — evaluate whether this aids spatial orientation without confusing it with addressing

tensions.actual: werk flush command implemented: writes tensions.json to workspace root with all tensions as raw structural data (id, short_code, desired, actual, status, parent_id, position, horizon, created_at), deterministic output (sorted by short_code, alphabetical keys), pretty-printed for git diff readability; autoflush deferred to v1.1
tensions.desired: tension tree state is flushed to a git-trackable file (JSON/JSONL) in the workspace — manually via a flush command and optionally via autoflush on mutation with debounce (à la beads_rust); git operations remain the users responsibility

tensions.actual: werk-web crate exists: Axum server with REST API (GET/POST/PATCH tensions, SSE live updates) calling sd-core directly via dedicated store thread. Interactive HTML frontend serves from 'werk serve' command — desired above reality card layout, gap visualization, live create/edit/resolve. Designed for Tauri migration: store-thread pattern maps to Tauri commands. No public deployment yet.
tensions.desired: a public web surface exists where others can view (part of) the tension tree and interact with it — prototype for demonstrating werk and inviting cooperation via Spikes or similar feedback mechanisms

tensions.actual: TUI fully wired into gesture/session model. Every TUI mutation (add, resolve, release, edit desire/reality/horizon, reorder, position, hold, note, undo, move) creates a gesture linked to the store session. Sessions start on TUI launch, end on drop. Epoch boundaries carry trigger gesture IDs. Children snapshots match CLI format (actual + position). Telemetry log correlates with store session ID. Multiple concurrent TUIs and CLI/MCP work correctly — each process holds its own session_id, no global state queries.
tensions.desired: epoch and session lifecycle are fully operational

tensions.actual: business foundation document is drafted covering economic thesis, market positioning, revenue architecture, and venture web; no revenue yet; no pricing validated; no public presence beyond statecraft.systems listing
tensions.desired: werk has a sustainable business model that funds continued development while remaining fundamentally true to the conceptual foundation

tensions.actual: nobody outside immediate circle knows werk exists; no public content, no posts, no demos
tensions.desired: werk is publicly known as the structural intent layer for agentic work — builders in the terminal-native AI ecosystem recognize it

tensions.actual: synthesis articulated in business foundation doc but not yet reflected in licensing, README, or release strategy
tensions.desired: the open-format / proprietary-instrument split is implemented and clearly communicated

tensions.actual: six revenue streams identified and reasoned through; none activated; practitioner license is first candidate
tensions.desired: at least one revenue stream is active and generating income

tensions.actual: integration points mapped in business foundation doc; no implementation; Statecraft website already lists werk as a tool
tensions.desired: werk integrates naturally with Statecraft services, Modern Minuteman operations, and Spikes feedback loops

tensions.actual: post drafted; video script drafted; neither published
tensions.desired: first public post about werk published — X post with Remotion video addressed to Jeffrey Emanuel and the agentic coding community

tensions.actual: concept articulated in business foundation doc; no implementation; no design doc
tensions.desired: coherence offering designed — one-liner or agent prompt that transforms existing workspace into a proposed werk structure

tensions.actual: Complete: reality/desire auto-create epochs, --no-epoch for minor corrections, epoch --show N queries all mutations (tension + descendants) within the epoch span. Ghost geometry builds through normal use and is queryable.
tensions.desired: Reality and desire updates create epoch boundaries — the ghost geometry builds through normal use, mutations are queryable per epoch span

tensions.actual: MCP server implemented. `werk mcp` starts stdio MCP server via rmcp. 30 tools registered across read (show, tree, list, survey, health, ground, diff, context, trajectory, insights), gesture (add, compose, reality, desire, resolve, release, reopen, move, hold, position, horizon, rm, snooze, recur), note (note_add, note_rm, note_list), epoch (epoch, epoch_list, epoch_show), and batch. All tools return structured JSON. Mutation tools trigger autoflush and hook infrastructure. Complex read commands delegate to CLI --json. Tested: initialize handshake, tools/list, show, tree, note_add all verified working.
tensions.desired: werk exposes its operative gestures as an MCP server

tensions.actual: No clipboard integration exists in TUI. User must manually retype or remember tension details when switching to an agent session.
tensions.desired: TUI yank — copy tension snapshots to clipboard for handoff to agent sessions

tensions.actual: All seven children resolved. Signal surfacing complete across all surfaces (show, list, TUI). Four design questions answered: #134 mutation log sufficient (no persistence), #135 --robot flag deferred (low priority, MCP is primary agent surface), #136 attention tracking out of scope (violates Standard of Measurement), #137 signal hooks deferred by design (event types exist, no consumer yet). Decisions are mutually consistent and grounded in sacred core.
tensions.desired: threshold detection as independent structural signal logic — urgency crossings, horizon drift, containment violations surfaceable without coupling to any invocation mechanism

tensions.actual: run command deleted (975 lines), watch command deleted (832 lines), run.rs test file deleted, agent-specific cross-area tests removed, which dependency removed. context command kept as general structural query tool. batch command preserved.
tensions.desired: run and watch commands deleted — agent orchestration removed from the instrument

tensions.actual: No staging mechanism exists. Batch command applies YAML mutations immediately. No concept of pending/draft mutations.
tensions.desired: staging mechanism — propose/pending/confirm/reject for asynchronous draft mutations. Multiple independent proposals, each separately confirmable/rejectable. For complex human restructuring and agent-proposed changes held for review.

tensions.actual: Foundation lists 12 operative gestures, all treating tensions as stable identity containers. Split/merge/restructure are performed ad-hoc through desire rewrites and fresh creates, damaging ghost geometry. No provenance field exists. Epochs are per-tension only — cannot represent phase transitions that span tension boundaries. The #25 split into #25+#49 demonstrated the cost: 6 incoherent epochs on #25, amnesiac #49.
tensions.desired: structural transformation gestures (split, merge, restructure) and cross-tension provenance — the foundation and data model support identity-topology-changing operations with lineage tracking

tensions.actual: Complete. Design rationale documented in designs/hook-infrastructure-design.md covering: five events, why these and not others (pre_create rejected, temporal events rejected per gesture principle, post_reopen deferred), payload semantics, firing rules per interface (CLI/MCP symmetric, TUI deferred), configuration model (workspace-scoped config.toml), execution model (pre blocks, post fire-and-forget), and explicit boundaries (hooks are notification surface, not extension mechanism).
tensions.desired: hook infrastructure has a clear design — rationale for when hooks fire, purpose of each event, firing rules across interfaces (CLI/MCP/TUI), pre/post symmetry, configuration model

tensions.actual: Conceptual foundation document amended (2026-03-27): terminology standardized (survey view, deck view, session), unimplemented features clearly marked as future with tension references (thresholds #19, split/merge #49, logbase #89, staging #48), stale concepts removed (over/under flags, aimed execution point, resolution velocity, cass reference), new concepts added (snooze, recurrence, three temporal orientations taxis/chronos/kairos, short codes, pathway palette independence insight), ⏱ glyph note added, StatusLine obsolescence decided. CLAUDE.md updated in prior commit. Two children resolved (#117, #121).
tensions.desired: werk has excellent documentation — the conceptual foundation is canonical, a user guide exists for practitioners at every stage, crate-level rustdoc is published, the CLI self-documents with examples, design decisions are traceable, and session learnings are captured in the right places

tensions.actual: All desired features complete: (1) usage examples in every --help — 23 commands; (2) non-interactive — health --yes, reality/desire fail-fast on no-TTY; (3) --dry-run on resolve/rm/move; (4) show redesigned from first principles — parent context, inherited deadlines, concise activity log; (5) note defaults to add — bare args work; (6) help grouped by framework via --help; (7) diff collapses position spam; (8) cross-command consistency via shared conventions. Premium CLI output achieved.
tensions.desired: CLI is ergonomic, forgiving, and self-documenting — accepts both positional and named args for common fields, note defaults to add, every command has usage examples in help text, help is grouped by framework not alphabetical, agents and humans get the same friction-free experience

tensions.actual: `reopen --reason` implemented. Records reason as a reopen_reason mutation within the gesture. Both CLI and MCP surfaces support it. Pattern follows release --reason.
tensions.desired: reopen gesture captures a reason — structural record of why something was brought back

tensions.actual: position command accepts single tension + position. No way to set a new order wholesale or partially in one gesture. Batch YAML supports SetPosition but no dedicated reorder command.
tensions.desired: batch positioning exists — reorder multiple tensions in one gesture

tensions.actual: Implemented. `tree <id>` shows the subtree under a specific tension. Works via Forest::subtree(). Both CLI and MCP surfaces support it.
tensions.desired: tree command can show a subtree under a specific tension

tensions.actual: Pathway palettes operational in TUI. Containment violation and sequencing pressure palettes surface as inline overlays after horizon edits, add-with-horizon, move, and reorder. j/k navigate options, Enter/number selects, Esc dismisses. Uses shared palette infrastructure from werk-shared. PATHWAY experiential state implemented.
tensions.desired: pathway palettes in TUI — inline option sets presented via ftui when gestures create structural signals (containment violations, sequencing pressure). Uses shared palette types from werk-shared. The TUI analog of CLI pathway palettes.

tensions.actual: Implemented. add command checks containment after child creation with horizon. move command checks both containment and sequencing after reparenting. Detection and presentation use existing shared infrastructure.
tensions.desired: palette triggers extended to add and move commands — add triggers containment palette when child created with horizon under deadlined parent, move triggers containment + sequencing after reparenting

tensions.actual: Implemented. Containment palette: keep, clip, extend, promote to sibling, remove deadline. Sequencing palette: keep, swap positions, move before, hold (remove position).
tensions.desired: expanded palette option sets — containment 3→5 options (+promote to sibling, +remove deadline), sequencing 2→4 options (+move before, +hold)

tensions.actual: Implemented. All four MCP tools (horizon, position, add, move) accept optional palette_response param. Signals returned in JSON; pre-selected action applied automatically.
tensions.desired: MCP tools accept optional palette_response parameter so agents can pre-select choices — horizon, position, add, move tools all return signals in JSON and accept pre-selected action

tensions.actual: Second response received from Skinner/Claude with: Mist routing model (5-tier), full Prism cycle on MRP thesis (6-model adversarial), and hook payload spec. Comprehensive reply drafted and sent covering: type corrections (urgency nullable/>1.0, trajectory enum, risk booleans), Prism response accepting Blue precision fixes, articulating creative-vs-reactive orientation thesis and anti-capture traceability, raising observational analysis design question. HookEvent enriched with parent_id and current_reality per spec. post_release hook added. Awaiting their survey --json reader and a real tension to run through Prism.
tensions.desired: Waterlight collaboration has a clear shape — what Mist gets from werk's structural urgency, what the hook contract looks like, and how AI composition (not functional coupling) handles the interpretive layer

tensions.actual: 22 tests in werk-shared/src/palette.rs covering: containment palette builder (5 options, descriptions, parent/child perspective), sequencing palette builder (4 options, order-matches-deadlines), containment applicators (dismiss, clip child, extend parent, promote to sibling, promote to root, remove deadline), sequencing applicators (dismiss, swap, move before, hold), edge cases (nonexistent tension, no deadline, no parent deadline, unpositioned, selected-0-as-dismiss, out-of-range selection). All pass.
tensions.desired: palette logic has test coverage — builders, applicators, detection helpers, and edge cases all tested against in-memory stores

tensions.actual: Cascading detection implemented in check_containment_after_horizon (werk-cli/src/palette.rs). After applying extend_parent, re-detects from that parent to catch upstream violations. Continues until no new violations found or MAX_CASCADE_DEPTH (5) reached. Visual separator shown in interactive mode. Only extend_parent triggers cascading — other choices reduce deadlines, escape the frame, or remove the deadline entirely.
tensions.desired: applying a palette choice that creates a new signal triggers cascading detection — palettes chain until the tree stabilizes or the user dismisses

tensions.actual: Decided and implemented. Standard of Measurement (#10) draws the line between metric and classification. Standard surfaces (context, show, hooks) expose engagement metrics — frequency, trend, gap samples, intervals — facts anchored to user actions. Classification (trajectory enum, risk flags) and projections live on analytical surfaces (trajectory command/tool, ground mode) framed as practice-layer analysis. Design note updated with resolution. Code changed: context returns engagement metrics not trajectory; trajectory MCP tool reframed; ground includes analytical trajectory section.
tensions.desired: observational analysis stance is decided — clear boundary between fact, signal, and dynamic in the instrument's output, with trajectory classification and projection layer each assigned to the correct side of that boundary

tensions.actual: no definition yet; Statecraft website lists werk as a tool but the integration shape is unspecified
tensions.desired: Statecraft integration point is defined — what a client session looks like when werk is the structural layer, shared tree vs export vs live view

tensions.actual: no definition; MM operates but werk isn't part of that workflow yet
tensions.desired: Modern Minuteman operations use werk as the planning instrument — what operations means here, whether it's the task backbone for MM content production

tensions.actual: no definition; Spikes exists as a concept but no signal pathway to werk is designed
tensions.desired: Spikes feedback loops connect to werk tensions — how audience signal maps back to structural tensions, whether via hook or ingest or manual

tensions.actual: three candidate paths identified: setup calls (service), donations (requires public presence first), build something obviously valuable (product). No validation on any path yet.
tensions.desired: first $1000 earned through practical means — proving someone will pay for what werk enables

tensions.actual: no offering exists; would need a price, a booking mechanism, and at least one person to pitch it to
tensions.desired: setup calls offered — install werk, walk through structural dynamics, configure a tension tree for someone's real project

tensions.actual: candidates: werk-as-claude-skill (zero install SD practice), hosted structural audit, productized tension tree session. Nothing built or validated.
tensions.desired: something obviously valuable is built and priced — a packaged product or skill that people encounter and want to pay for

tensions.actual: depends on #37; no public visibility yet beyond statecraft.systems listing
tensions.desired: public presence exists sufficient for donations/sponsors to flow

tensions.actual: Conceptual foundation implemented and operational. Three interface surfaces: TUI (V1-V8 complete, Console V2 Phase 1 landed, primary experience functional), CLI (30+ commands with foundation vocabulary, JSON on all commands, pathway palettes), MCP (30 tools via rmcp/stdio). Data model: gestures, sessions, epochs, temporal calculus, frontier projection, hook infrastructure, advisory locking, pathway palettes. No-op mutation guard and health --repair added. Cooperative dynamics remain unexplored (pending Foundations course April 2026). Business model in exploration (first $1000 target set, three paths identified, no revenue).
tensions.desired: palette works

tensions.actual: etest
tensions.desired: test

tensions.actual: Conceptual foundation implemented and operational. Three interface surfaces: TUI (V1-V8 complete, Console V2 Phase 1 landed, primary experience functional), CLI (30+ commands with foundation vocabulary, JSON on all commands, pathway palettes), MCP (30 tools via rmcp/stdio). Data model: gestures, sessions, epochs, temporal calculus, frontier projection, hook infrastructure, advisory locking, pathway palettes. No-op mutation guard and health --repair added. Cooperative dynamics remain unexplored (pending Foundations course April 2026). Business model in exploration (first $1000 target set, three paths identified, no revenue).
tensions.desired: new item

tensions.actual: V1-V8 complete. Notes gesture polish (#76): TextInput widget for annotating mode (cursor movement, word nav), byte-safe UTF-8 truncation in note display, adaptive note sizing (no hardcoded cap — rendering fills available space). Accumulated zone now heterogeneous — AccumulatedItem enum interleaves parent notes with resolved/released children by timestamp, selectable/focusable. Post-annotate auto-focuses detail card. Focus zoom redesigned (#94 resolved). Diamond glyph vocabulary. Open: root-level epochs (#89/#90), input interface unification (Adding/Searching still use bare input_buffer).
tensions.desired: TUI architectural consolidation — unified cursor, single frontier per frame, deck at root, remove old renderer

tensions.actual: hogwash
tensions.desired: testing

tensions.actual: CreatorTools.net researched in depth — charges $130/month for course + simple charting tool with telescoping. Community mapped: ~50-200 active practitioners, 2 certified instructors. Moritz registered for FST April 2026, has email thread with Kira & Katharina (fst@robertfritz.com). Draft outreach email to Katharina composed. Business foundation document updated to reorient around SD practitioners as primary market. Web UI prototype and Tauri tech choice made for the premium product.
tensions.desired: werk is the standard practice tool for structural dynamics practitioners and coaches — the successor to what Fritz once offered, and the obvious thing to charge for

tensions.actual: CreatorTools.net (Ivan Fritz, Robert's son) still operates at $130/month — offers 'structural tension charts' that are essentially goal-tracking forms (desired outcome, current reality, 12 action steps with checkboxes). No hierarchy, no dynamics, no temporal logic. LifeStudio iPad app preceded it (~2015), discontinued ~2019. A PDF chart template also exists. The tool is weak, not gone — which is better for us: the market is paying for something werk already surpasses
tensions.desired: Fritz's discontinued tool is researched — what it was, what it did, what practitioners relied on, what's missed, and how werk can surpass it

tensions.actual: no practitioner outside Moritz has seen werk; Nicholas Catton is Fritz-influenced but not confirmed formally certified — runs coaching in San Diego; the two certified Fritz instructors are Katharina Schutze (Berlin, since 2004) and Kira Higgs (Portland, since 2019); Fritz Japan practice also exists; no outreach attempted yet
tensions.desired: SD practitioners and coaches have validated werk as useful for their practice — at least three have tried it and given feedback

tensions.actual: CreatorTools.net pricing: $130/month, $428/3-months, $1,300/year — for a course + simple charting tool. FST training: 4-day live online (price unlisted). SCCP: $125/quarter workshops + $150/critique. This establishes a price anchor — practitioners already pay $130/month for far less than what werk offers structurally
tensions.desired: werk is packaged and priced for coaches — per-seat, per-client licensing that fits how coaching practices actually work

tensions.actual: Tauri 2.0 desktop app exists and opens natively on macOS. werk-app/src-tauri/ has the Rust backend (store actor, 8 commands), werk-app/src-web/ has the adapted HTML frontend. Full tension tree loads, all CRUD gestures work through native IPC. werk-web Axum server also works (werk serve). Two GUI paths operational: native Tauri app and browser via Axum. Not yet a practice tool — needs polish, deeper SD feel, step-level actions, proper icon/bundling.
tensions.desired: werk has a graphical interface — browser or native Mac app — that non-terminal users find inviting and natural to use, and that serves as the premium product

tensions.actual: Tauri selected. Research documented. Architecture validated: werk-web's store-thread channel pattern maps directly to Tauri commands. Ready to resolve once Tauri scaffold exists and proves the pattern.
tensions.desired: the GUI technology choice is made — web app, Tauri, native SwiftUI, or other — with clear rationale for the SD practitioner audience

tensions.actual: Live interactive web UI at werk serve. Create tension, evolve desire, update reality, resolve, reopen, decompose (add child) — all via API calls, no terminal needed. Search with auto-expand, filter by status. SSE auto-refreshes on any mutation. Missing: step-level actions (resolve/edit individual children from the UI), horizon management, position/reorder, release, notes. Not yet at parity with the core practice loop.
tensions.desired: the GUI reaches feature parity with the core practice loop — create tension, see the structure, gesture through it, track dynamics

tensions.actual: Spatial law enforced: desire at top, theory of closure as vertical bridge, reality at bottom. Warm palette, generous spacing, no terminal aesthetic. Structural dynamics vocabulary in the UI (evolve desire, update reality, decompose, theory of closure). Bridge line connects children visually from desire toward reality. But still feels more like a list than a structure you inhabit. Needs: better gap feel, step-level interaction, descended view that makes the theory of closure a reasoning surface not just indented items.
tensions.desired: the GUI has visual design that expresses structural dynamics — not a generic task manager skin but something that makes the practice feel right

tensions.actual: Conceptual foundation implemented and operational. Three interface surfaces: TUI (V1-V8 complete, Console V2 Phase 1 landed, pathway palettes operational, primary experience functional with known architectural debt being addressed), CLI (30+ commands with foundation vocabulary, JSON on all commands, pathway palettes), MCP (30 tools via rmcp/stdio). Data model: gestures, sessions, epochs, temporal calculus, frontier projection, hook infrastructure, advisory locking, pathway palettes. No-op mutation guard and health --repair added. Cooperative dynamics remain unexplored (pending Foundations course April 2026). Business model in exploration (first $1000 target set, three paths identified, no revenue).
tensions.desired: test

tensions.actual: Substantial implementation complete. Data model: gestures, sessions, epochs tables; gesture_id + actual_at on mutations; parent_snapshot_json on child creation. Temporal calculus: 6 computed properties in temporal.rs. Frontier projection: operating envelope in frontier.rs. Hook infrastructure: 5 hook types with JSON payloads. Pathway palettes: containment/sequencing signals independent of invocation. Advisory file locking for fsqlite safety. State machine: 8+ states implemented implicitly in TUI (InputMode enum), not formally specified. Epoch boundaries auto-created on CLI reality/desire updates. Remaining gaps: formal state machine extraction (#16 — may not be needed), TUI epoch triggers (#30), cross-temporal threshold monitoring (#46), structural transformations (#49), observational analysis stance decision (#65).
tensions.desired: test

tensions.actual: not started — root level currently shows no accumulated zone and no epoch boundary
tensions.desired: logbase — a searchable, queryable substrate of all prior desire/reality/epoch layers across the entire field, accessible from root view and all surfaces (CLI, MCP, TUI, web, desktop, mobile)

tensions.actual: Design inquiry complete. Three design docs produced: command-center-and-time-axis.md, visualization-research.md, three-orientations.md. Key decisions: (1) 2D lattice (structure x time) with stream/survey/frontier as zoom presets. (2) Three temporal axes: taxis, chronos, kairos. (3) NOW zone = command center embedded in survey. (4) Tab is binary stream<->field-wide, with survey/frontier as sub-choice. Survey V1 is implemented and functional. Frontier/kairos view designed but not built.
tensions.desired: the root level is a command center — structural overview, temporal vitals, logbase access, mission preparation — where stream (deck) and survey (time-first) orientations collapse into one surface

tensions.actual: not started — desire/reality are currently non-selectable fixed zones. Shift+Enter is a V9 placeholder. Prior epochs are only indicated as a count in the bottom bar.
tensions.desired: cursor can select desire and reality anchor zones — enables orient zoom (Shift+Enter) for full-text expand, and cursor-through to prior epochs below reality

tensions.actual: broken — Store::init() takes an advisory file lock for the entire session. CLI commands fail with 'store is locked' while TUI is open. Lock should wrap individual write transactions, not the whole session.
tensions.desired: advisory lock is write-scoped, not session-scoped — CLI commands should be able to read and write alongside a running TUI

tensions.actual: Tauri 2.0 scaffold complete in werk-app/. Store actor pattern (dedicated OS thread for !Send Store, mpsc channels). 8 Tauri commands: get_tree, create_tension, update_desired, update_reality, resolve_tension, reopen_tension, get_tension, position_tension. Web frontend adapted from werk-web — uses __TAURI_INTERNALS__.invoke() for all IPC. Compiles clean, opens a native macOS window, loads and displays the full tension tree. Creating/updating tensions works live. No Tauri CLI installed yet (cargo run -p werk-app works directly).
tensions.desired: werk ships as a Tauri desktop app — the web frontend wraps in a native window, Axum server replaced by Tauri command layer

tensions.actual: Detail card implemented: Focus (Enter) shows reality text in subdued weight, metadata line (intent→bridge→trace: ⏱ deadline, child count, born/◆ desire/◇ reality ages), recent notes. Peek (Space) shows lighter card. Focus/Peek switch between densities. Diamond glyph vocabulary: ◆ cyan route, ◆ green next, ✧ subdued held, ◇ subdued reality, ⏱ deadlines everywhere. Reconciliation protects focused items. Root suppresses accumulated. Reorder keeps held separate. e edits anchors, Enter reserved for focus.
tensions.desired: focus zoom shows a detail card (full desire/reality text, temporal facts, recent notes, child count breakdown) — not a miniature descent

tensions.actual: not started — no mobile technology choice made. Tauri 2.0 supports mobile targets (iOS/Android) which could share the web frontend. Native Swift/Kotlin is the alternative. The logbase and all surfaces (CLI, MCP, TUI, web, desktop, mobile) should share the same queryable substrate.
tensions.desired: werk has a mobile app — iOS and/or Android — that brings the practice into the pocket, with touch-native gestures for the core loop

tensions.actual: unspoken intent since the beginning. werk -g currently just means a workspace at ~/.werk. The deeper vision: global workspace knows about all registered local workspaces. Cross-field patterns (where is energy going, which fields are stagnant). If root level of a single workspace is a command center for that field, global is the command center for all fields. Connects to logbase (cross-workspace query), survey view (cross-field temporal overview), and the mobile app (which field am I looking at right now).
tensions.desired: the global workspace is a meta-command-center across all workspaces — register, view, filter, flit between fields, query the logbase across all of them

tensions.actual: Every command now has after_help examples showing real invocations with short codes. Covers: add, compose, epoch, horizon, show, reality, desire, resolve, release, reopen, snooze, recur, rm, move, hold, position, note, health, list, tree, context, diff, nuke. Examples use short codes throughout (no ULIDs).
tensions.desired: every --help includes 2-3 usage examples — agents pattern-match off examples faster than descriptions

tensions.actual: health --repair now accepts --yes to skip confirmation; without --yes and no TTY, fails fast with actionable error and correct usage. reality/desire commands detect no-TTY when value is omitted and fail fast with usage hint instead of trying to open $EDITOR. All three interactive prompts eliminated for non-interactive contexts.
tensions.desired: CLI is fully non-interactive — no stdin prompts, no $EDITOR fallback; every input passable as a flag, fail-fast when no TTY detected

tensions.actual: resolve, rm, and move all support --dry-run. Validates all inputs (prefix resolution, status checks, cycle detection) then previews the operation without mutating. Both human and JSON output include dry_run flag. rm dry-run additionally shows children that would be reparented.
tensions.desired: --dry-run on all destructive single commands — agents preview before committing

tensions.actual: short codes work within a single workspace; no addressing scheme for cross-workspace references; ULIDs remain internal primary keys but are never needed at the user level
tensions.desired: cross-workspace addressing scheme exists — workspace-prefixed short codes (e.g. werk:42, @global:42) distinguish tensions when looking across workspaces without exposing ULIDs to the user

tensions.actual: Entire palette is hardcoded for dark backgrounds in theme.rs. Six RGB colors (CLR_DEFAULT through CLR_GREEN) plus CLR_SELECTED_BG and CLR_BG are all dark-mode values. clear_area_styled now uses TRANSPARENT bg (terminal default) instead of explicit black — first step toward light mode. ftui has no built-in theme detection. Light terminals show invisible white-on-white text, alien dark selection blocks, and generally unusable UI.
tensions.desired: werk renders well on both dark and light terminal backgrounds — adaptive color palette, readable text, appropriate selection highlighting across terminal themes

tensions.actual: clear_area_styled uses TRANSPARENT bg (done). CLR_BG (black) still used in CLR_SELECTED_BG definition and potentially in deck rendering. Need to audit all .bg() calls in theme.rs, render.rs, deck.rs, survey.rs.
tensions.desired: all explicit background colors replaced with TRANSPARENT — terminal native background shows through everywhere except intentional highlights

tensions.actual: No detection exists. Options: COLORFGBG env var (many terminals set it), OSC 11 background query, --light/--dark CLI flag, or WERK_THEME env var. ftui has no built-in detection.
tensions.desired: terminal theme detected at startup — Dark or Light enum available to palette construction

tensions.actual: Single hardcoded dark palette. CLR_DEFAULT (220,220,220) is invisible on white. Light mode needs dark text (30,30,30), medium dim (140,140,140), adjusted accents.
tensions.desired: dual palette in theme.rs — STYLES constructed conditionally based on detected theme, dark mode unchanged, light mode readable

tensions.actual: CLR_SELECTED_BG is rgb(35,35,42) — a dark blue-gray. On light terminals this creates an alien dark block. Needs a light variant (rgb 220,225,235 or similar) keyed to detected theme.
tensions.desired: selection highlight adapts to terminal background — visible and subtle on both dark and light

tensions.actual: CLR_CYAN (80,190,210) and CLR_GREEN (80,190,120) likely work on both. CLR_AMBER (200,170,60) may need darkening for light. CLR_RED (220,90,90) is fine. Needs visual verification, possibly light-mode variants.
tensions.desired: accent colors verified readable on both dark and light backgrounds

tensions.actual: Redesign complete and shipped: parent shows truncated desired, inherited horizon walks ancestor chain with clear attribution, Status/Created/Position/Last act compacted, mutations replaced by Activity section (most-recent-first, concise summaries like 'reality updated' and 'positioned at 3' instead of full text dumps), creation mutation just says 'created', signals section only appears by exception, null position displays fixed
tensions.desired: show command output is premium — information hierarchy serves the reader, parent shows context, inherited deadlines visible, activity log is concise and most-recent-first, no redundant data

tensions.actual: All four children resolved. Shared conventions: display_id_named() and format_timestamp() in werk-shared. Mutations: add shows parent context, Status:Active removed, release shows child count, diff uses short codes + concise summaries. Reading: diff overhauled. Utility: note list relative timestamps, nuke tension count. show redesigned (parent from #107). format_mutation_summary shared between show and diff. Cross-cutting consistency achieved.
tensions.desired: every CLI command output is premium — consistent formatting, information hierarchy, structural context, no redundancy, agent-friendly and human-friendly, most-recent-first temporal data

tensions.actual: display_id_named() added to werk-shared — shows #code + truncated desired for structural context. format_timestamp() added — relative for <7d, compact date for older. Both exported from werk-shared. show.rs already uses display_id_named pattern for parent display. These are the conventions; remaining work is adopting them across all commands.
tensions.desired: shared output conventions enforced — consistent ID display (#code everywhere), consistent timestamps (relative <7d, date for older), parent context helper, compact KV layout helper

tensions.actual: add now shows parent with context (#code — truncated desired). Status:Active removed from creation output. release shows active child count. diff uses short codes and concise mutation summaries (reuses format_mutation_summary from show). note list uses relative timestamps via format_timestamp. nuke preview shows tension count.
tensions.desired: mutation commands have consistent, concise output — success line with structural context (parent shown as #code Name), no redundant fields (omit Status:Active on creation), pathway signals shown inline

tensions.actual: diff now uses short codes (#N) instead of raw 8-char ULID prefixes and concise mutation summaries via shared format_mutation_summary. Remaining: survey urgency column (would require significant rendering rework), epoch show box-drawing (visual polish), ground epoch timestamps (minor). These are lower-priority polish items — the core inconsistencies are fixed.
tensions.desired: reading commands have polished, scannable output — survey shows urgency, diff uses #code format, epoch show uses box-drawing for nesting, ground groups gestures meaningfully, timestamps are consistent

tensions.actual: note list now uses relative timestamps via format_timestamp (<7d relative, older dates as compact date). nuke preview shows tension count before confirmation prompt. batch markers reviewed — [ok] for dry-run, [done] for applied, [FAIL] for errors — clear enough in context. config path display would require plumbing changes to the config system — deferred.
tensions.desired: utility commands are clean and informative — note list uses relative timestamps, nuke preview shows tension count, batch uses clear status markers, config shows file path

tensions.actual: Arg rewriting in main.rs: when note is followed by something that's not add/rm/list/--help, injects 'add' automatically. werk note 42 text, werk note text, and werk note add 42 text all work. Explicit subcommands unchanged.
tensions.desired: note defaults to add — werk note 88 "text" works without explicit subcommand

tensions.actual: Framework grouping added as after_long_help on the CLI root. Shows 5 groups: Structure (add, compose, move, rm, show, tree, context), Action (reality, desire, resolve, release, reopen, hold, position, note), Time (horizon, snooze, recur, epoch, diff), Framing (list, survey, ground, trajectory, insights, health), System (init, config, flush, batch, nuke, mcp, serve). Visible via --help (long), short -h shows flat list with 'see more with --help' hint.
tensions.desired: top-level help grouped by framework — Architecture of Space, Grammar of Action, Calculus of Time, Logic of Framing, system

tensions.actual: Consecutive position mutations collapsed: runs of 3+ position changes show as single line with count (e.g. 'positioned at 1 (28 position changes)'). Runs of 1-2 shown normally. Non-position mutations break runs. Reduced diff output significantly — #4 tension went from 28 lines to 1.
tensions.desired: diff output is concise — collapses repeated position mutations, limits mutations per tension, shows summary not exhaustive log

tensions.actual: Four polish items identified in CLI output audit: (1) diff needs --compact or per-tension limit for multi-day windows, (2) epoch show nesting uses flat indentation — box-drawing would improve readability, (3) survey has no urgency column unlike list, (4) config get/set doesn't show which file was modified
tensions.desired: CLI polish pass — diff compact mode, epoch box-drawing, survey urgency column, config file path display

tensions.actual: CLAUDE.md updated with two new sections: CLI Conventions (--json, --help with examples, framework grouping, non-interactive mode, --dry-run, note shorthand, short codes) and CLI Output Design Principles (7 principles from show redesign: first-principles thinking, information hierarchy, no redundancy, most-recent-first, honest attribution, compact layout, shared conventions). Future sessions will reference these.
tensions.desired: CLAUDE.md reflects current CLI capabilities — help grouping, examples, non-interactive mode, dry-run, show redesign principles, note shorthand, shared output conventions

tensions.actual: onboarding.md covers first 5 minutes. troubleshooting.md covers common issues. dynamics-guide.md covers interpretive frameworks. No guide for the intermediate practitioner — the gap between 'I created my first tension' and 'I have 50 tensions and use epochs'
tensions.desired: a practitioner's guide exists — covers the first tension through scaling to complex multi-level structures, when to epoch, how to read the frontier, pattern recognition, and common anti-patterns

tensions.actual: 30 tools listed in SKILL.md with categories. No usage guide showing how an agent should interact with werk — when to use context vs show, batch vs individual gestures, how to read the frontier and act on it
tensions.desired: MCP integration guide exists — how to connect agents, which tools to use for what, patterns for agent-driven structural dynamics practice

tensions.actual: code-level docs in every lib.rs and most modules. cargo doc likely works but not published or hosted. No inline examples in doc comments.
tensions.desired: crate-level rustdoc is generated and accessible — each public API documented with examples

tensions.actual: The CLI output design principles are now captured in CLAUDE.md under 'CLI Output Design Principles' — the canonical location that every session reads first. The 7 principles (first-principles thinking, information hierarchy, no redundancy, most-recent-first, honest attribution, compact layout, shared conventions) are durable instructions that future agents will follow. A separate designs/ document isn't needed — CLAUDE.md is the right home for operational principles that guide implementation.
tensions.desired: design principles from implementation sessions are captured in canonical docs — CLI output principles, structural dynamics vocabulary decisions, what's sacred and why

tensions.actual: All 3 children resolved. Runtime artifacts gitignored (#123), distribution assets moved to local/ (#124→#125), all crates on edition 2024 (#124). Repo tracks only source, designs, and config.
tensions.desired: repository is clean, well-organized, and intentional — every tracked file has a clear reason, runtime artifacts are gitignored, crate boundaries are respected, root is minimal

tensions.actual: .beads/ and .factory/ added to .gitignore. Both directories removed from git tracking (git rm --cached). 138 files untracked. Local files preserved — only git index affected.
tensions.desired: .beads/ and .factory/ runtime artifacts gitignored — only configuration tracked, not generated state

tensions.actual: All 7 crates standardized on edition 2024. ftui (FrankenTUI) requires it — the nightly dependency was already real, now it's consistent. Two trivial ref-pattern fixes for edition 2024 migration (deck.rs, config.rs).
tensions.desired: Rust edition choice is intentional and documented — either standardize on 2021 or document why sd-core needs 2024

tensions.actual: video/ (25 source files) moved to local/video/, research/archive/ (17 files) moved to local/research/. Both untracked from git. local/ is gitignored. Video-specific .gitignore lines replaced with single local/ entry. Remotion still works from local/video/ — all paths are relative. No files deleted.
tensions.desired: large assets (video/, research/archive/) have a clear storage strategy — either git-lfs, separate repo, or documented acceptance of the weight

tensions.actual: Hooks exist in .githooks/ and .claude/ but must be manually copied. Need: werk init --hooks or werk hooks install that sets up git hooks (pre-commit flush + README update) and Claude Code hooks (UBS scan) for the current repo, plus a --global flag for user-wide Claude settings.
tensions.desired: CLI command to install werk hooks into a repo

tensions.actual: Persistent intermittent issue across multiple sessions. Mitigations applied: bold removed from selected style, per-line rendering, clear_area_styled with TRANSPARENT bg, all Cell::default() paths eliminated, Bayesian diff strategy disabled for deterministic full diff. Reduced but not eliminated. Root cause is interaction between ftui diff engine strategy selection and terminal state drift. Nuclear option (force FullRedraw every frame) not yet tried.
tensions.desired: the TUI never exhibits rendering corruption — no white glitch, no black screens, no SGR state bleed, regardless of scroll position, terminal emulator, or focus events

tensions.actual: Three threads identified and held together in designs/addressing-sessions-gestures.md. Exploratory — no commitments. Key open questions: addressing syntax, gesture/telemetry boundary, session as knowledge substrate vs separate model. Converges with LogBase (#89), ground mode, authority, and multi-player provenance.
tensions.desired: deep addressability, expanded gesture vocabulary, and session semantics form a coherent design

tensions.actual: Option A implemented: no separate persistence table. The mutation log is the source of truth. detect_horizon_drift() now computes onset timestamp — the mutation that tipped the drift pattern. show displays 'since N days ago' on drift signals. list --signals filter shows only tensions with active signals. Signal computation gated behind --long/--signals/--json in list (no cost for default list). Onset for other signal types (critical path, containment, sequencing) deferred — would require historical forest reconstruction.
tensions.desired: signal state persistence and cross-query change detection

tensions.actual: show displays drift in Signals section with ↝ glyph (type, count, net shift). list computes signals (behind --long/--signals/--json): ! overdue, ‡ critical path, ↥ containment, ⇅ sequencing, ↝ drift. list --signals filter shows only signaled tensions. Legend at bottom. JSON includes horizon_drift in show and signals array in list. Noise threshold: only RepeatedPostponement/Oscillating drift shown in list.
tensions.desired: horizon drift surfaced as structural signal — when a deadline moves, the fact of movement (direction, magnitude) appears at the tension

tensions.actual: TUI survey renders signal glyphs (\! ‡ ↥ ⇅ ↝) inline between desire text and ID column. Signals computed during survey load via Forest + compute_temporal_signals. Drift noise threshold: only RepeatedPostponement/Oscillating. Glyphs styled amber. Field vitals bar shows 'N signaled' count. Both provider lines and tree-child lines render signals.
tensions.desired: TUI renders structural signals inline — overdue, containment, sequencing pressure, critical path, horizon drift appear by exception at the tension

tensions.actual: v1.4.0 released. Conceptual foundation amended (standard of measurement, operative identity). CLI reading surface consolidated: 10 commands to 4 primary (show/list/tree/stats). Output taxonomy settled: engagement metrics on standard surfaces, classification on analytical. MCP stats tool added. Old commands hidden but functional.
tensions.desired: next release shipped — output taxonomy breaking change documented, engagement metrics replace projection on standard surfaces

tensions.actual: MCP surface updated. New stats tool with sections parameter (temporal, attention, changes, trajectory, engagement, drift, health, all). show tool gains full parameter (ancestors, siblings, engagement — absorbs context). list tool schema gains new filter params (overdue, approaching, stale, held, positioned, root, parent, has_deadline, changed_since). Old MCP tools (health, ground, diff, context, trajectory, insights, survey) still functional. README MCP section updated. Implementation complete across all three remaining steps: show --full, tree --stats, MCP update.
tensions.desired: CLI reading surface is clean — show, list, tree, stats cover all read needs; ground/insights/trajectory/health/survey/context/diff dissolved into these four

tensions.actual: decided: mutation log is sufficient, no persistence layer. Signals remain a pure function of (mutations, now). Scaling math shows no near-term concern (10K mutations = sub-second). If hooks (#137) are later needed, compute-on-write at mutation time rather than a snapshot table. Purity guarantee — no hidden state that could diverge — is worth more than pre-computed convenience. Consistent with Standard of Measurement: instrument computes from what user provided.
tensions.desired: should signal state be persisted separately or is the mutation log sufficient as the single source of truth for computing signals at any point in time

tensions.actual: decided: add --robot as a separate flag (additive, not breaking --json). Envelope pattern {ok, data, error, elapsed_ms} consistent with btv/cass/dcg/sc ecosystem. Low priority — MCP is the primary agent surface with its own protocol envelope. --robot serves CLI-as-agent-tool case, which is secondary. Defer implementation until an agent integration actually needs it.
tensions.desired: should the CLI adopt a --robot envelope pattern (ok/data/error/elapsed_ms) for agent consumption beyond the current --json raw data output

tensions.actual: decided: attention tracking is out of scope. Tracking 'when user last looked' introduces an instrument-originated standard of measurement, violating principle #10. Creates a managerial surveillance dynamic contradicting operative identity. Existing mechanisms suffice: --changed (user-supplied timeframe) and --stale (user-supplied threshold) are both anchored to user standards.
tensions.desired: does temporal attention tracking (when did the user last look, what changed since) belong in the signal system or is it a separate concern

tensions.actual: decided: deferred by design. Event types already exist (UrgencyThresholdCrossed, HorizonDriftDetected) but no consumer exists. No daemon, no webhook infra, MCP queries on demand. When a consumer appears (TUI live updates, werk watch), the path is clear: compute-on-write in grammar layer, emit existing event types, subscribe in consumer. Building infrastructure without a consumer violates design discipline.
tensions.desired: should signal-aware hooks fire on signal transitions and if so what is the trigger mechanism

tensions.actual: Survey: zero-padded to fixed 2 digits, no # prefix (e.g. '02', '42'). Deck: zero-padded to variable width (2 or 3 based on max short_code), no # prefix (e.g. '042'). Both omit #. Inconsistency is fixed vs variable width. Zero-padding in deck could also be dropped for cleaner display.
tensions.desired: survey and deck views use consistent shorthand ID formatting — survey zero-pads to fixed 2 digits, deck pads to variable width based on max short_code

tensions.actual: thresholds are currently hardcoded or caller-supplied (list --approaching takes a duration, list --stale takes days). No user-persisted configuration for signal surfacing breakpoints. Anchored to Standard of Measurement #10: the instrument should not originate thresholds — the user should supply them.
tensions.desired: user-defined signal thresholds — the practitioner sets when signals surface (approaching window, stale duration, urgency glyph breakpoints) rather than hardcoded defaults

tensions.actual: First integration delivered: FrankenNetworkX (#141 resolved). DiGraph substrate in Forest, four structural signals (centrality, waves, longest path, descendants), surfaced across CLI/MCP/TUI. Phase 2 design doc written for typed edges. Remaining: #142 FrankenMermaid, #143 FrankenSearch, #144 fsqlite MVCC, #145 ftui, #146 FrankenPandas, #147 FrankenEngine, #148 FrankenWhisper.
tensions.desired: werk integrates the full FrankenSuite — graph intelligence, visual output, hybrid search, deeper storage and TUI, analytics, sandboxed extensions, voice input

tensions.actual: Phase 1 complete: Forest wraps DiGraph (option 3a), four structural signals computed (centrality, waves, longest path, descendants), surfaced in CLI show (◉ HUB / ┃ SPINE / ◎ REACH glyphs), MCP show (JSON), TUI survey (glyphs). 503 tests pass. Phase 2 design doc written (designs/structural-graph-overlay.md) covering typed edges, link/unlink gestures, combined graph signals.
tensions.desired: FrankenNetworkX is the graph substrate inside Forest — DiGraph lives alongside the existing HashMap (option 3a), forest.graph() exposes it, betweenness centrality / topological waves / longest path / transitive descendants are computed as StructuralSignals, surfaced in show/MCP/TUI/web with ◉ HUB / ┃ SPINE / ◎ REACH glyphs by exception, ComplexityWitness preserved for ground mode

tensions.actual: blocked — FrankenMermaid exists (github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenmermaid) but depends on unpublished franken-kernel (local path only). Cannot integrate until franken-kernel is publicly available. No visual output on any surface.
tensions.desired: FrankenMermaid renders tension structures visually — flowchart/mindmap for web and desktop, SVG export from CLI, braille/block minimap in TUI, auto-generated diagrams for docs and public content, gantt/timeline for temporal views

tensions.actual: Core delivered across all three surfaces. TUI: FrankenSearch-backed / search, active-only, ~62μs per keystroke, zero DB reads. CLI: --search flag on list, ranks by relevance. MCP: search tool for agents. In-memory index with cached metadata, rebuilt on mutation. Decomposed into 6 children: #156 search gestures, #157 inline filters, #158 hybrid BM25 upgrade, #159 logbase indexing, #160 relevance context, #161 quality embedder.
tensions.desired: FrankenSearch provides hybrid retrieval across all werk content — BM25 for TUI search, full two-tier (lexical + semantic + RRF fusion) for the logbase, semantic queries via MCP let agents search by meaning not just structure

tensions.actual: MVCC concurrent writers implemented. PRAGMA fsqlite.concurrent_mode=ON set on all connections. BEGIN CONCURRENT replaces BEGIN at all 6 transaction sites. Commit-with-retry (exponential backoff, 10 attempts) replaces advisory file lock. WriteLockGuard, acquire_lock(), write_lock(), fs4 dependency all removed. 503 tests pass. CLAUDE.md updated. Backup logic retained (RaptorQ not in v0.1.2).
tensions.desired: fsqlite upgraded to use MVCC concurrent writers — the advisory file lock is removed, multiple processes safely write to the same store via BEGIN CONCURRENT with retry-on-conflict. RaptorQ self-healing deferred (not in v0.1.2).

tensions.actual: ftui v0.2.1 confirmed: 77 widgets, 19 layout modules, 48 runtime modules available. Full API audit complete. Imaginal sketch written (designs/tui-reimagined.md). Dead code cleaned (temporal_indicator string, trail, gap_bar removed). Current TUI: 7,884 lines hand-rolled rendering inside ftui Program shell. Theme is 8 hardcoded colors. Bayesian diff disabled. 10 input modes instead of modals. No undo, no persistence, no responsive layout.
tensions.desired: the TUI is reimagined on ftui — PaneLayout spatial skeleton, focus graph navigation, modal interaction, widget-native rendering, adaptive theme, reactive state, undo, responsive breakpoints, session persistence. See designs/tui-reimagined.md

tensions.actual: stats are hand-computed aggregation; projection engine uses simple trend lines; export is basic JSON/text; no DataFrame capability
tensions.desired: FrankenPandas powers analytics — stats command uses DataFrames for pivot tables, rolling windows, cross-tabulations; trajectory analysis uses proper statistical functions; export supports Parquet and rich formats; coach dashboards have real data processing

tensions.actual: hooks are shell commands via event bus; batch mutations are YAML replay; no sandboxing, no deterministic replay, no provenance tracking; cross-workspace sync does not exist
tensions.desired: FrankenEngine sandboxes extensions — user-defined hooks run in a deterministic runtime with cryptographic receipts, batch mutations are programmable scripts not just YAML, cross-workspace sync carries verified provenance

tensions.actual: no audio input on any surface; all mutation input is typed text; mobile app (#95) not started; coaching workflow (#80) is manual
tensions.desired: FrankenWhisper enables voice-driven practice — speak reality updates, coach sessions transcribed and parsed into mutations, mobile app uses voice as primary input, thinking-out-loud sessions captured as structural observations

tensions.actual: fnx-classes, fnx-algorithms, fnx-runtime added as git deps from github.com/Dicklesworthstone/franken_networkx — compiles clean, pulls mwmatching transitively
tensions.desired: fnx-classes, fnx-algorithms, fnx-runtime added as git dependencies in sd-core/Cargo.toml — compiles clean on nightly with edition 2024

tensions.actual: Forest struct holds graph: DiGraph field, built in from_tensions() with parent→child edges, exposed via forest.graph(). All 24 existing tests pass. PartialEq and Default implemented manually (DiGraph doesn't derive them).
tensions.desired: Forest holds a DiGraph alongside its HashMap — from_tensions() builds both, forest.graph() exposes &DiGraph, all existing Forest methods and tests unchanged

tensions.actual: sd-core/src/graph.rs created with StructuralSignals, ComputationWitness, FieldStructuralSignals, compute_structural_signals(). Runs centrality, waves, longest path, descendant count. 5 new tests (empty, single, chain, fan, diamond). All 466 tests pass.
tensions.desired: sd-core/src/graph.rs exists with StructuralSignals, ComputationWitness, compute_structural_signals() — centrality, waves, longest path, descendant count computed from forest.graph()

tensions.actual: show command displays ◉ HUB / ┃ SPINE / ◎ REACH by exception with threshold filtering (centrality > 0.0001, descendants > 5). Wave shown in identity block. structural field in JSON. MCP show includes structural signals. TUI survey shows ◉ and ┃ glyphs. Web deferred (no per-tension signal display yet). All 503 sd-core tests pass.
tensions.desired: show command displays ◉ HUB / ┃ SPINE / ◎ REACH structural signals by exception, wave in identity block, structural field in JSON output — MCP show includes structural signals — TUI survey shows structural glyphs — web API includes structural signals

tensions.actual: designs/structural-graph-overlay.md written. Covers: 3 edge types (enables/competes/informs), edges table schema, link/unlink gesture semantics, combined graph construction, new full-graph signals (community detection, centrality shift, competing pairs, enabling chains), conceptual foundation amendment, evidence ledger integration, open questions.
tensions.desired: Phase 2 design document in designs/ covers typed edge semantics, edges table schema, link/unlink gestures, combined graph construction, new full-graph signals, and conceptual foundation amendment for cross-cutting relationships

tensions.actual: Evaluation completed 2026-03-31. Reframing as 'operative observations' is partially valid (no enforcement differs from blocking) but partially rationalization (the topology and computation are management regardless of vocabulary). Violates locality principle. Marginal value at current scale (70 active tensions). Premature — other FrankenSuite work (#142, #143, #144, #145) delivers more value sooner. Design doc preserved for future revisit when scale warrants it. Repositioning lower under #140.
tensions.desired: Typed cross-cutting edges implemented — edges table in store, link/unlink gestures in CLI/MCP/TUI, combined graph (containment + typed edges) fed to FNX algorithms, new signals from full graph (community detection, competing pair collision, enabling chains)

tensions.actual: tree renders: ID, status marker, horizon, OVERDUE/EXCEEDS_PARENT/PRESSURE, desired text, closure progress. No position/route. No structural signal glyphs. No staleness indicator. Sort is horizon-only, not canonical children_by_horizon() ordering. The show command displays all these but tree does not.
tensions.desired: tree command shows richer per-node signals — position/route indicator, structural signal glyphs (HUB/SPINE/REACH), staleness, and sorts by canonical ordering (position DESC then deadline) instead of horizon-only

tensions.actual: Search navigates to the selected tension on submit. No in-overlay gestures yet.
tensions.desired: TUI search acts on results — gestures from the search overlay (resolve, annotate, move, yank) without navigating first

tensions.actual: Search is content-only. Structural filtering requires the list command or separate navigation.
tensions.desired: TUI search filters inline — type status:resolved or parent:10 to narrow results by structural properties alongside content

tensions.actual: Hash embedder only (features=["hash"]). Upgrade path: change to features=["hybrid"] in Cargo.toml. FrankenSearch's TwoTierSearcher activates BM25+RRF automatically. Disk index needed (persist_disk_index).
tensions.desired: search upgraded to hybrid retrieval — BM25 lexical via Tantivy alongside hash embedder, RRF fusion ranks results from both tiers

tensions.actual: Only tension desired+actual text is indexed. Notes, mutations, epochs, design docs are not searchable. Depends on logbase (#89) existing as a content store.
tensions.desired: search indexes notes, mutations, epochs, and design docs — the logbase substrate makes all prior decisions retrievable by meaning
